classifiers.name: Finite
classifiers.notes: Character (property vs structure) computed from the leg, not stored

classifiers.name: Graded
classifiers.notes: Chosen grading data; forgetful generally not full

classifiers.name: Domain
classifiers.notes: Domain ≠ IntegralDomain; need not be commutative

classifiers.name: WithBasis
classifiers.notes: Morphisms are arbitrary R-linear maps (addendum Rmk 9.3 case 1): the chosen basis is structure on objects and imposes no condition on maps. 'Modules with basis' never determines its morphisms, and the two conventions give different categories -- case 2, basis-preserving maps, is equivalent to Set. Sage is case 1, checked: in CombinatorialFreeModule(QQ,['a','b']) the map a |-> 2*W['x'], b |-> 0 sends basis vectors to non-basis vectors and is a morphism of the category.

classifiers.name: Limits
classifiers.notes: Foundations Rmk 14.4: existence of limits is property-like. CHOSEN limits are a different, structure-like classifier with nontrivial fibres; if a consumer needs a choice it must say so.

classifiers.name: Free
classifiers.notes: Freeness is the essential image of the free R-module functor F_R (addendum Def 9.1, Cor 9.2), equivalently the propositional truncation of the existence of a chosen basis (Rmk 13.7) -- hence a property classifier, not structure. The finite restriction F_R|FinSet has the finite free modules as its image.

classifiers.name: Monoidal
classifiers.notes: Foundations Def 14.2: MonCat -> Cat. The fibre over a fixed category can contain SEVERAL inequivalent monoidal structures, so a consumer must name the chosen lift -- (x)_R or (+) on R-Mod are different lifts of the same category. Unnamed structure-reference is the standing lift-ambiguity hazard.

classifiers.name: WithBasis
classifiers.notes: refined from named-join path | Morphisms are arbitrary R-linear maps (addendum Rmk 9.3 case 1): the chosen basis is structure on objects and imposes no condition on maps. 'Modules with basis' never determines its morphisms, and the two conventions give different categories -- case 2, basis-preserving maps, is equivalent to Set. Sage is case 1, checked: in CombinatorialFreeModule(QQ,['a','b']) the map a |-> 2*W['x'], b |-> 0 sends basis vectors to non-basis vectors and is a morphism of the category.

classifiers.name: WithBasis
classifiers.notes: authored sagecats ledger | Morphisms are arbitrary R-linear maps (addendum Rmk 9.3 case 1): the chosen basis is structure on objects and imposes no condition on maps. 'Modules with basis' never determines its morphisms, and the two conventions give different categories -- case 2, basis-preserving maps, is equivalent to Set. Sage is case 1, checked: in CombinatorialFreeModule(QQ,['a','b']) the map a |-> 2*W['x'], b |-> 0 sends basis vectors to non-basis vectors and is a morphism of the category.

classifiers.name: Commutation
classifiers.notes: Foundations Def 85.3: on an object of BiAct(R,S) the two composites R (x) M (x) S -> M, r(x)m(x)s |-> (rm)s and r(x)m(x)s |-> r(ms), agree. A PROPERTY classifier -- the full replete locus is _R Bimod_S. So the two actions are structure and their commutation is an additional diagrammatic property, not part of the data.

classifiers.name: Preadditive
classifiers.notes: Foundations Def 14.1: a preadditive category is enriched in abelian groups, and the forgetful map from categories with a CHOSEN preadditive structure is a structure classifier.

classifiers.name: Additive
classifiers.notes: Foundations Def 14.1: finite biproducts, a limit condition stated RELATIVE to a chosen preadditive structure.

classifiers.name: Abelian
classifiers.notes: Foundations Def 14.1: kernels and cokernels with every mono and epi normal. 'Being abelian is a property' is always understood relative to the additive enrichment that makes the axioms meaningful.
